properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This 3-4 car parking area provides direct access to the Cohutta Wilderness area via the Chestnut Lead trail.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take I-75 north to I-575.  Take I-575 north.  This turns into Hwy 515/Hwy 5.  Continue to Ellijay.  In Ellijay, take GA Hwy 52 west.  Approximately 9 miles outside of Ellijay, turn right just past 9-mile Church at the sign for Lake Conasauga, onto Conasauga Road.  Continue 1.3 miles to a road intersection where the pavement ends.  Take the right fork onto Shake Rag Road and drive about 2.5 miles to another intersection.  Turn left onto Wilderness Trail Road (which is Forest Road 90) and go 1.7 miles.  Turn right onto Forest Road 68 (sign points to Lake Conasauga) and travel 3.3 miles to the T-intersection at the top of the mountain.  Turn left following Forest Road 68 and go 2.2 miles to Chestnut Lead Trailhead on the right.  To reach Tearbritches Trail, continue on Forest Road 68 another 1.5 miles (trail on the right side of the road, parking on the left).

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Near the Lake Russell Campground, this 100-acre lake is easy to access via grass beach area, boat ramp, group pavillon, and the adjacent Lake Russell Wildlife Management Area. There are numerous hiking and biking trails including Lake Russell Loop Trail, Sourwood Trail, and Nancytown Day Use Trailhead. Ladyslipper Equestrian is located nearby.
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset

properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Yes, drinking water is available from faucets

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION:
To reach the mini-rapids and broad flat water of the scenic Chattooga River in this location, follow the gently sloping 0.3-mile gravel hiking/portage trail that descends through mixed hardwood and pine forest in the quiet, fresh mountain air.  Watch for deer, snakes, and other wildlife.
Earl's Ford is the take-out for boaters on Section II and the start of Section III of the Chattooga Wild and Scenic River.  This is where the whitewater on this river truly begins.  All boaters must wear a life jacket and inner tubing is prohibited on the river below Earl's Ford.  Boaters must complete free self-registration permits to float or paddle the Chattooga; permits are available at the parking area for this access.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, drive northwest on SC 28 and turn left onto Whetstone Road (193). Drive 5 miles and cross Chattooga Ridge Road (196) where Whetstone becomes Earls Ford Road. Continue another 4 miles to the parking area at the end of the Earls Ford Road. The trail is at the end of the parking area.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: ELLICOTT ROCK WILDERNESS WEST (Trailhead)

Located on State Highway 107, this picnic area is situated in a hemlock and rhododendron grove along the East Fork of the Chattooga River.  There is a restroom in the parking lot, and several picnic tables are scattered throughout the site and along the river.  This location also provides hiker access to the Foothills Trail as well as the Fork Mountain Trail, which enters nearby Ellicott Rock Wilderness.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, SC, follow State Hwy 28W for 8 miles, then veer right to continue on State Hwy 107 for 14 miles.  The picnic area will be on the left just before the NC/SC border.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This 6-8 vehicle parking area provides access to three Cohutta Wilderness trails;  Rice Camp, Hickory Ridge, and East Cowpen. High clearance vehicles are recommended as there are two creeks that must be forded on the access road leading to the trailhead.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take I-75 north to Exit #293, Hwy 411, Chatsworth/White.  Go north on Hwy 411 through Chatsworth. At the Hardee's, continue on Hwy 411 north about 12.3 miles to Cisco. Turn right at the Cisco Baptist Church onto Old GA Highway 2 and drive about 8.1 miles.  Turn right onto Forest Road 51 (sign here that says Rice Camp, East Cowpen, Hickory Creek, Horseshoe Bend) and go 2.8 miles to the Horseshoe Bend Trailhead on the left, a big open area.  To reach the Rice Camp/East Cowpen/Hickory Creek Trailhead, continue straight on Forest Road 51 another 2.1 miles to the end of the road at the trailheads.  NOTE:  There are 2 stream fords on Forest Road 51 which may not be passable with smaller cars.  Do not stop in the middle of the streams but continue moving at a slow but steady speed.  After heavy rainfall, the stream fords will rise, and crossing is not recommended.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This remote gravel parking area can hold up to 20 vehicles and provides access to the Cohutta Wilderness area via the highly popular Beech Bottom trail.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take I-75 north to Exit #293, Hwy 411, Chatsworth/White.  Go north on Hwy 411 through Chatsworth.  At the Hardee's, continue on Hwy 411 north about 12.3 miles to Cisco. Turn right at the Cisco Baptist Church onto Old GA Highway 2 and drive about 9 miles to the Jacks River Bridge at the Tennessee state line.  Cross the bridge, turn right and go about 1.2 miles.  Turn right onto Forest Road 62 (Big Frog Loop Road) and go about 4.7 miles to the trailhead parking area on the left.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This remote 10-15 car parking area, no amenities, access to Cohutta Wilderness area via Jacks River and Hemptop trails.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site

properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take I-75 north to I-575.  Take I-575 north.  This turns into Hwy 515/Hwy 5.  Continue to Blue Ridge, Georgia.  In Blue Ridge, turn left onto Hwy 5 at McDonalds.  Travel approximately 4 miles to GA Hwy 2 on the left.  Turn left onto GA Hwy 2 and continue for about 10.5 miles.  Pavement ends, road turns to dirt.  At the Watson Gap 4-way intersection, turn right onto Forest Road 22.  Intersection is well signed with arrow to right for Dally Gap.  Drive about 3.5 miles to Dally Gap parking lot.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: The Dry Creek Trail system offers 26 miles of hiking, biking, and horseback riding awesomeness. The trailhead provides an accessible horse rider loading ramp, vault toilet facilities and a multi-table covered picnic pavilion. Gravel parking for 30 horse trailer rigs.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per vehicle per day
$25 per vehicle per annual
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset
properties.PASSES: Annual Forest Pass available and may be purchased at the district office, by mail with a check, or by phone with a credit/credit card.

properties.DIRECTIONS: :  Take Exit 320 on I-75; follow State Highway 136 west about 14 miles to Villanow, Ga. At the intersection with Highway 201, turn left onto East Armuchee Road (County Road 705) and go about 7 miles. Just past the signed Manning Mill Road, turn left onto Forest Road 226 and follow the signs to the Dry Creek Trailhead.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: A fabulous day-hiking opportunity for young and old awaits visitors at the Keown Falls Recreation Area.  With picnicking, dramatic vistas and a hike to the Keown Falls and overlook that even Grandma can handle, this little gem is sure to become one of your favorite waterfall viewing spots.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Cradled in the hills of the North Georgia Mountains overlooking beautiful Lake Rabun, this developed recreation area offers hiking, all sorts of water activities like swimming, boating, and fishing, and an opportunity to absorb the peaceful lake scenery.
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: ANGEL FALLS (Trailhead) located in Rabun Beach Campground. Fee tube, kiosk, toilets, picnic table, grill at the main trailhead. There is a fee free limited size parking area with a single sign that will access the trail located on Lake Rabun Road. This fee free parking is only parking during closure times of the campground.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per vehicle per day
properties.PASSES: Day use fee charged by Concessionaire for parking at the official trailhead parking site.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: The Davenport Mountain OHV Trail is 5.9 miles in length. The trails are suitable for motorcycles and ATVs. This trail loops around Davenport Mountain with some moderate grades, and offers views of Nottely Lake. This trail is approximately 12.0 miles west of Blairsville, GA. This trail has an easy section, which is recommended for young or inexperienced riders. There are also sections that are more difficult. Riders must stay on the OHV trail; cross-country riding or riding on the main road is not allowed. The trail crosses a road at two places. Watch for other vehicles on the road at these crossings. Davenport Mountain hours of operation are from sunrise to sunset. A fee is required to ride on this trail.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per vehicle per day
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset
properties.PASSES: Purchase a Digital Day Use Pass: https://www.recreation.gov/activitypass/f74700af-77c0-11eb-86df-e60b5d20d0e4
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Beginning at the traffic light at the intersection of US 76/Highway 515 and US 19/129, go west on US 76/Highway 515 for 7.0 miles. Turn right onto GA 325 and go 2.3 miles to the STOP sign. Turn left, still on GA 325, and go 2.8 miles and turn right onto Davenport Mountain Road. At the foot of the hill, turn right onto Forest Service Road #143. Follow the gravel road 1.1 miles to the parking lot on your right. The OHV trail begins at the back end of the parking lot.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Cooper Creek Campground is located in the Chattahoochee-Oconee National Forest along the banks of Cooper Creek in the North Georgia Mountains. The Campground offers many recreation activities including camping, hiking, trout fishing, and hunting. The campground is well kept and is the perfect place for both weekend getaways or extended stay. Most of the campsites are fairly spaced apart.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Frank Gross is a U.S. Forest Service Campground located approximately 2 hours north of Atlanta in the North Georgia Mountains of the Chattahoochee National Forest. Frank Gross Campground offers visitors year-round recreation opportunities such as camping, fishing, picnicking, and hiking. Trout fishing is popular here in North Georgia and this campsite adjacent to Rock Creek is an amazing opportunity for avid fisherman.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Cedar Creek Rifle Range accommodates targets at 25, 50, and 100 yards. This range is fully accessible to disabled visitors, and three of the five shooting tables are under a shelter.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take Whetstone Road (located .25 mile north of the ranger station) to Cassidy Bridge Road. Turn left onto Cassidy Bridge Road and take it several miles down to Cedar Creek Rifle Range Rd. Turn left and follow this road, stay left at the fork and the road ends at the range.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Named for a former mayor of Salem and located approximately 25 miles north of Walhalla, the Oscar Wigington Scenic Overlook offers a spectacular view of northeastern Oconee and northwestern Pickens counties. Scanning the rolling foothills one can see several lakes, hilltops and other landmarks.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, there are two ways to reach the overlook:1. Travel toward Highlands, NC on State Highway 28 to State Highway 107 toward Cashiers. Turn right on State Highway 413 and the overlook is about 1/2 mile on the right.2. Travel north on State Highway 11 to State Highway 130 and turn left. Turn left on State Highway 413 and the overlook is about 2 miles on the left.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: WHITEWATER FALLS (Observation Site)
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$3 per vehicle per day
$15 per annual
properties.PASSES:
$3/vehicle fee, $15 annual pass (usable at Dry Falls, Whiteside Mountain, and Whitewater Falls)&

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This 6-8 vehicle parking area provides access to the Cohutta Wilderness via Conasauga River trail.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take I-75 north to I-575.  Take I-575 north.  This turns into Hwy 515/Hwy 5.  Continue to Ellijay.  In Ellijay, take GA Hwy 52 west.  Approximately 9 miles outside of Ellijay, turn right just past 9-mile Church at the sign for Lake Conasauga, onto Conasauga Road.  Continue 1.3 miles to a road intersection where the pavement ends.  Take the right fork onto Shake Rag Road and drive about 2.5 miles to another intersection.  Turn left onto Wilderness Trail Road (which is Forest Road 90) and go 1.7 miles.  Turn right onto Forest Road 68 (sign points to Lake Conasauga) and travel 3.3 miles to the T-intersection at the top of the mountain.  Turn right onto Forest Road 64 and drive 1.4 miles to Betty Gap Trailhead on the left.  To reach Three Forks Trailhead, continue on Forest Road 64 another 3 miles to the parking area on the left side of the road.

Deep Hole offers visitors year-round recreation opportunities such as camping, fishing, picnicking, and a launch area for tubing and kayaking along the Toccoa River.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Grapevine Campground is located along the banks of the Chauga River, one of the popular fishing streams on the district.  It is open year round with no charge and site amenities include fire rings, lantern posts, and a vault toilet.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: Located at the end of Grapevine Branch Rd/Forest Service Rd 764 off Chattooga Ridge Road, approximately 1.5 miles south of the intersection with Whetstone Road.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This was a busy self-sufficient Appalachian farmstead in the late 1800s and early 1900s. It is listed in the National Register of Historic Places, and an interpretive sign on the site details the original layout of the farmstead.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, SC, follow SC Highway 28 north to Cuzzin's General Store. Take the left fork (Hwy. 28). The farmstead is on the left before the bridge crossing the Chattooga River into Georgia.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION:
Nestled in a mixed pine/hardwood stand, this picnic area is beautiful any time of year. Picnic tables are scattered about, and a Civilian Conservation Corps stone and wood picnic shelter and a restroom are accessible by wheelchair from the lower loop parking lot. Yellow Branch creek is located across from the picnic area and is easily reached by a short loop hiking trail.
The Yellow Branch Falls Trail departs from the picnic area.  This 1.3 mile (each way) moderately difficult out-and-back trail leads you to the base of a spectacular 50-foot vertical cascade.  Remember to wear sturdy shoes and bring plenty of water, and be aware of your physical abilities: the hike back is uphill all the way!
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only; no overnight camping allowed
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, SC, drive about six miles north on Highway 28. The picnic area is on the left.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Whetstone Horse Camp is near the Chattooga Wild and Scenic River and serves as a base camp for riders on both the Rocky Gap (South Carolina) and the Willis Knob (Georgia) horse trails. Site amenities include drinking water, fire rings, tables, lantern posts, hitching rails and highlines, and vault toilet facilities.

Single Site: $15 per night 
Double Site: $30 per night
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Check in: 4:00 p.m.; Check-out: 2:00 p.m.; Quiet hours are 10:00 p.m. until 6:00 a.m.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: The Cassidy Bridge Hunt Camp is located in a nicely wooded area along the banks of the Chauga River, one of the popular fishing streams on the district. During big-game and turkey hunting seasons, the campground is opened free of charge. During the rest of the year, the camp is available by reservation.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Overnight Use:
Group Site: $30 per night
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day free of charge annually during hunting seasons 10/1-12/13 and 4/1-4/30; reservable remainder of year: Check in: 3:00 p.m.; Check-out: 2:00 p.m.
properties.PASSES: Reservations can be made by visiting: https://www.recreation.gov/camping/campgrounds/234450 OR Please be prepared to pay with CASH or CHECK. *Sites may be reservable and reservation fees apply.*
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, SC, drive 5 miles north on Highway 28. Turn left on Whetstone Road, and follow it for three quarters of a mile to Cassidy Bridge Road. Turn left on Cassidy Bridge Road, and follow it for 5 miles to the campground.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This much admired 17-site developed campground is located at the junction of Tallulah River and Tate Branch in the North Georgia mountains. Whether traveling to the forest to camp, fish, hike, or just breathe clean mountain air, this remote location offers visitors a chance to escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday living and unwind. In addition, the site has 4 walk-in tent sites which allow campers and hikers to take full advantage of the area's rustic character.

properties.DIRECTIONS: Take State Hwy 441 north 3 miles to Boggs Mtn Connector. Left on Boggs, go to OLD 441, turn left then second right onto Stonewall Rd, (FSR 20). Road dead ends at parking lot.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: POCKET PICNIC AREA (Picnic Site)
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per vehicle per day
$25 per annual
properties.PASSES: Chattahoochee-Oconee National Forests: $25/annual. https://www.fs.usda.gov/detail/conf/passes-permits/?cid=fseprd529794 Interagency Recreation Passes are Honored. store.usgs.gov/recreational-passes Please be prepared to pay with CASH or CHECK.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: GLEN FALLS TRAILHEAD (Trailhead)
WESTFORK CAMPGROUND has 5 campsites located in the campground and public access to the Chattooga River W&S on designated paths
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Overnight Use:
Single Site: $10 per night
properties.PASSES: Interagency Senior/Access Recreation Passes provide a 50% discount to Single Campsites ONLY. *The discount applies only to the fee for the campsite physically occupied by the pass holder, not to any additional fee or campsite(s) occupied by members of the pass holder's party.* https://store.usgs.gov/recreational-passes Please be prepared to pay with CASH or CHECK. *Sites may be reservable and reservation fees apply.*

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: Also commonly referred to as "Low Water Bridge Campground", this quiet primitive campground is located on the banks of the Chattooga River with fishing, boating, and tubing opportunities nearby. It is open year-round with no charge and site amenities include trash cans, fire rings, and a vault toilet.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, SC, follow State Hwy 28W for 15 miles, then turn left on Low Water Bridge Rd.  The campsites are located along the road opposite the Chattooga River.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This location provides parking and access to the Chattooga Wild and Scenic River for boaters, tubers, and fishermen. Amenities include a parking lot, self-registration boating permit station, boat launch, and vault toilet.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Walhalla, SC, follow State Hwy 28W for 16 miles.  The river access will be on the left.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: ELLICOTT ROCK WILDERNESS EAST (Trailhead)

The thrill of driving off-road vehicles over mountainous terrain and trail systems equals the Oakey Mountain OHV recreation area. This site near Lake Burton and Lake Rabun offers two-way trails that are open to OHVs with less than a 48-inch axle width and offers terrain from intermediate to rocky and steep. All OHV trails on the national forest close during wet conditions when trail use can cause damage to trails and erosion into nearby streams.  Please contact the Chattooga River Ranger District for the latest information on trail availability due to frequent weather contingencies. 706-754-6221 or check the website www.fs.usda.gov/conf
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per operator per day
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset
properties.PASSES: Purchase a Digital Day Use Pass: https://www.recreation.gov/activitypass/3b2cbeb3-77bf-11eb-86df-e60b5d20d0e4 OR Download the Recreation.gov mobile app prior to your trip so you can pay the fee on-site with Scan and Pay: https://www.recreation.gov/mobile-app
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No drinking water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Clayton, GA take US-76 west for 7.0 miles.Turn left onto Charlie Mtn. Road and drive 3.6 miles. Turn right onto Bridge Creek Road and go 2.0 miles. Turn right onto Burton Dam Rd. and go 1.6 miles.  Turn left onto Forest Service Road 161 and arrive at Oakey Mtn. OHV trailhead.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This 6 car parking area provides access to Cohutta Wilderness via the Jacks River trail.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take I-75 north to Exit #293, Hwy 411, Chatsworth/White.  Go north on Hwy 411 through Chatsworth.  At the Hardee's, continue on Hwy 411 north about 12.3 miles to Cisco. Turn right at the Cisco Baptist Church (across from the Cisco Mart Gas Station) onto Old GA Hwy 2 and drive about 9 miles to the Jacks River Bridge at the Tennessee state line.  Cross the bridge, turn right and go about 100 feet to the trailhead on the right.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: TUMBLING CREEK CG
Day use site with access to a waterfall with viewing platform about .1 mile up easy  trail.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take GA Hwy 75 north from Helen for approx. 8 miles, just beyond mile marker 15, turn left onto Chattahoochee River Road (FSR 44) and follow it for 4.5 miles. Trailhead is at far end of campground.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: BLUE VALLEY SCENIC OVERLOOK (Observation Site)

Just west of Dalton lies the largest Forest Service OHV system of trails in Georgia.  These very popular motorized trails offer something for everyone, from easy to difficult, in a wooded setting.  This 25 mile system is for dry riding, so it closes after significant rain events to allow the trails to dry and limit resource damage, sometimes for several days.  Be sure to call the Conasauga Ranger District before driving to the area.  On weekends and at night, call 706-695-6736 ext. 2 for closure recording.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per operator per day
$50 per operator per year
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Open 24 hours/day
properties.PASSES: . Download the Recreation.gov mobile app prior to your trip so you can pay the fee on-site with Scan and Pay: https://www.recreation.gov/mobile-app
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: From I-75, take Exit 336 Dalton/Rocky Face.  Go north on US Hwy 41 for approximately 3 miles to intersection with GA Hwy 201.  Turn left on GA Hwy 201, go to second road on the right, Houston Valley Road.  Turn right on Houston Valley Road and continue through two intersections.  Go 1 mile past the second intersection and turn left on Capehart Road.  Continue for 1 mile to dead end at parking lot for Houston Valley.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: The Jake and Bull Mountain Trail System provides approximately 36 miles of recreational trails for equestrians, bicyclists and hikers.  The Jake Mountain Trail System and Bull Mountain Trail System connect to form a complex system of scenic trails within a four square mile area of the Chattahoochee National Forest's Blue Ridge Ranger District.  This area is located in western Lumpkin County, less than ten minutes from downtown Dahlonega.  The system is made up of interconnecting dirt trails and gravel roads which are accessed by two trailheads.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.PUBLIC_SITE_NAME: Jake And Bull Mountain Trail System
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: From Blue Ridge, Follow US-76  13.6 miles to State Rd 52.  Turn left onto State Rd 52 E for 26 miles.  Turn left onto Nimblewill Church Rd. and follow 2 miles to access road (Forest Service Road 299) for Jake Mountain Parking Lot.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: The Whissenhunt OHV Trail is 11.2 miles in length with all one-way traffic trails. The trails are suitable for motorcycles and ATVs, less than 50 inches wide. The trail system is approximately 6.0 miles north of Dahlonega, GA. Users are required to stay on the signed trails to prevent damage to the surrounding resources. Whissenhunt hours of operation are from sunrise to sunset. A fee is required to ride on this trail system.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:
Day Use:
$5 per vehicle per day
$50 per operator per annual
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: Day use only: open sunrise to sunset
properties.PASSES: Purchase a Digital Day Use Pass: https://www.recreation.gov/activitypass/f5baf41e-77a8-11eb-86df-e60b5d20d0e4
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: No water is available
properties.DIRECTIONS: Take US 19/60 north from Dahlonega for 2.2 miles. Turn left on paved county road (Wahsega Road) toward Camp Frank D. Merrill. Go 3.9 miles to dirt Forest Service Road #662, turn left into OHV parking area.

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: The Beasley Knob OHV Trail System is a challenging trail system located off of U.S. Highway 76 in Union County, GA.  It is conveniently located two miles east of the town of Blairsville and can be accessed by two trailheads.  The system provides approximately 13.4 miles of recreational driving for OHV enthusiasts , including those riding four-wheel drive vehicles, motorcycles and ATVs. The system is made up of interconnecting dirt trails, rock hill climbs and gravel roads through the Chattahoochee National Forest.  It is recommended for experienced riders, only, due to its challenging terrain and steep ascents.  Please, no bolted, studded or tire chains allowed
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION:

properties.RECAREA_DESCRIPTION: This picnic area features a stone and timber picnic shelter built in the 1930s by the Civilian Conservation Corps and a curving boardwalk winding through the cool streamside forest past picnic spots and a fishing pier.  Hikers can access the East Fork Trail leading into the Ellicott Rock Wilderness from the picnic area.  Visitors here can also access the adjacent Walhalla State Fish Hatchery where South Carolina Department of Natural Resources raises brown and rainbow trout to stock local watersheds.  Site amenities include picnic tables, small fire pits/grills, and vault toilet facilities.
properties.FEE_DESCRIPTION: No fees are required for this site
properties.OPERATIONAL_HOURS: 6 am to 10 pm (year round)
properties.WATER_AVAILABILITY: Water can be treated or filtered from nearby water sources
properties.DIRECTIONS: Nine miles north of Oconee State Park on Highway 107. Follow signs to the Walhalla State Fish Hatchery.
